results.rendered_html:
saṃ-√rabh Ā. -rabhate, (pr. p. -rambhamāṇa,  MW. ; Impv. -rambhasva,  Bālar. ),
to seize or take hold of, mutually grasp or lay hold of (for dancing &c.), grasp, grapple each other (in fighting &c.),  RV. ;  AV. ;  ŚBr. ;
to get possession of (instr.),  R. ;
to grow excited, fly into a passion,  MBh. ;  Kāv.  &c.

results.rendered_html:
saṃrabh 1 A.
1 To become agitated, be overwhelmed or affected.
2 To be exasperated or furious, be enraged or irritated (mostly in p. p.);  R. 16. 16. 
3 To seize, catch hold of (Ved.)
